✽ F

● Ha — you're completely right, I fire AskUserQuestion, not you. So I can run a controlled capture myself: I'll ask a
  real question now (with this paragraph before it), which renders the menu in this session's PTY and surfaces it to  
you.   Once you answer, I'll read this room's live PTY dump to see exactly how the prose sits relative to the menu — and
  build the extraction against that real render instead of guessing. This also live-demonstrates the bug: you'll likely
  see the buttons first and this paragraph land afterward.

  ⏵⏵ bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle) · esc to interrupt

  early:

✶ Skedaddling… (1m 6s · ↓ 4.2k tokens)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 ☐ Prose fix 

Once I extract Claude's explanatory paragraph from the live screen and show it before the question, what should happen 
to the clean copy that still arrives from the transcript after you answer?

❯ 1. Keep it (safety net)  
   Show the extracted paragraph before   the question AND s ill let the transcript copy through after. Lowest risk — you
     never lose the full paragraph even if the screen capture is partial — but you may see it twice.
  2. Suppress the duplicate
     Show the extracted paragraph before, and drop the post-answer transcript copy so there's no repeat. Cleaner
     timeline, but if the screen capture is truncated (very long prose) you'd lose the tail.
  3. Smart: suppress only if full
     Suppress the post-answer copy only when the pre-answer capture looks complete; otherwise let the transcript copy
     through. Best of both, but more logic to get right.
  4. Type something.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  5. Chat about this

Enter to select · ↑/↓ to navigate · Esc to cancel
(B
